panels.description: Wide establishing shot: A grand, ornate ballroom in Netherfield, glowing chandeliers, guests milling about, Mr. Bingley stands in the center looking cheerful and planning the dance, period Regency attire, soft warm lighting.

bubbles.text: The invitation arrives, and Bingley considers the night ahead with delight.

panels.description: Medium shot: Mr. Collins standing stiffly, gesturing obsequiously toward Elizabeth. He wears a formal black clerical coat, his face is self-important and earnest, background features the ballroom edge.

bubbles.text: I shall hope to be honoured with the hands of all my fair cousins... I take this opportunity of soliciting yours, Miss Elizabeth, for the two first dances.

panels.description: Close-up: Elizabeth's face showing sudden surprise, brows slightly arched, eyes wide. Her expression is complex, shifting from shock to cautious skepticism.

bubbles.text: I am not the better pleased with his gallantry, from the idea it suggests of something more.

panels.description: Medium shot: Elizabeth looking pensive while Mr. Collins hovers in the background, making exaggerated bows. The Hunsford setting is implied by an architectural archway.

bubbles.text: Mr. Collins might never make the offer, and, till he did, it was useless to quarrel about him.

panels.description: Wide shot: The Bennet sisters looking out of a large window at a bleak, rain-swept landscape. Grey sky, mud-slicked paths, feeling of restriction and boredom, anticipation of the ball.

bubbles.text: The dreary weather kept them trapped inside, awaiting the distant promise of the Netherfield ball.
